Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 4, under '3 RESULTS', it states: 'Of these, all 1003 patients with metastatic disease were analysed here: 502 (50%) allocated to standard treatment (SOC-alone group) and 501 (50%) to standard treatment plus abiraterone and prednisolone (SOC + AAP group) (Figure 1).' The 'SOC-alone group' is the control arm, with 502 participants. This is further confirmed by Figure 1 and Table 1 on page 5, both showing 'SOC-alone n = 502'.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: On page 3, in the 'What's new?' section, it states: 'Median follow-up increased from 52 to 73 months.' Also, on page 6, in the first paragraph, it explicitly states: 'Median follow-up was 73 months (6.1 years).'

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 4, in the '3 | RESULTS' section, it states: 'Of these, all 1003 patients with metastatic disease were analysed here: 502 (50%) allocated to standard treatment (SOC-alone group) and 501 (50%) to standard treatment plus abiraterone and prednisolone (SOC + AAP group) (Figure 1).' Figure 1 and Table 1 on page 5 also confirm 'Allocated to SOC+AAP N = 501'.

Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The abstract on page 2 states: "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE... Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy." Page 3 further clarifies: "The comparison randomised patients 1:1 to SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP)..." The arms are also clearly labeled as "SOC-alone" and "SOC + AAP" in Figure 1 and Table 1 on page 5.

Control Arm.evidence: The Abstract on page 2 states: "Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy." Additionally, on page 4, section 2.2 "Randomisation and masking" mentions: "Allocation was 1:1 to standard-of-care (SOC-alone) only group or SOC-alone with abiraterone acetate and prednisolone/prednisone group (SOC + AAP)." This indicates that SOC-alone is the control arm.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The title of the article states "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patients starting hormone therapy". The abstract also mentions "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP)" as the treatment that demonstrated improved survival. In the 'Randomisation and masking' section on page 4, it is stated that patients were allocated to "SOC-alone with abiraterone acetate and prednisolone/prednisone group (SOC + AAP)". The 'Procedures' section on page 4 further specifies that patients in the SOC + AAP group received "abiraterone (1000 mg daily) with prednisolone (5 mg daily)".

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 3, under the 'RESULTS' section, it states: "One thousand and three patients were contemporaneously randomised (November 2011 to January 2014)". This is further clarified on page 4, under '3 RESULTS', which states: "Between 15 November 2011 and 17 January 2014, 1917 patients were randomised to the arms of STAMPEDE constituting the 'abiraterone comparison'. Of these, all 1003 patients with metastatic disease were analysed here: 502 (50%) allocated to standard treatment (SOC-alone group) and 501 (50%) to standard treatment plus abiraterone and prednisolone (SOC + AAP group) (Figure 1)." The CONSORT diagram on page 5 also confirms 'Metastatic disease at randomisation N = 1003', split into 'Allocated to SOC N = 502' and 'Allocated to SOC+AAP N = 501'.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The comparison randomized patients 1:1 to SOC-alone (control arm) or SOC-alone with abiraterone acetate and prednisolone (experimental arm), as stated in the first paragraph under 'Funding information' on page 3 and confirmed in section 2.2 'Randomisation and masking' on page 4, and section 3 'RESULTS' on page 4, and Figure 1 on page 5.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Abbreviations' section, 'QOL' is defined as 'quality of life'. On page 10, in the 'AUTHOR CONTRIBUTION' section, the text states: 'Direct comparison of patient-related QoL outcomes previously supported the use of abiraterone over docetaxel...'. This indicates that Quality of Life (QoL) outcomes were reported.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document mentions 'QOL, quality of life' in the abbreviations section on page 1 and refers to 'patient-related QoL outcomes' in the discussion on page 8, referencing another paper (citation 14). However, this document does not explicitly state which standardized questionnaire or survey was used to assess the participants' overall well-being and satisfaction with their health status, nor does it report any specific quality of life scale results within its content.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Found in Table 1 'Baseline characteristics' on page 5, under 'Metastatic disease risk group', row 'High-risk', and column 'SOC + AAP (n = 501)'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Found in Table 1 'Baseline characteristics' on page 5, under 'Metastatic disease risk group', row 'High-risk', and column 'SOC-alone (n = 502)'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Found in Table 1 'Baseline characteristics' on page 5, under 'Metastatic disease risk group', row 'Low-risk', and column 'SOC-alone (n = 502)'.

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is explicitly stated in the title of the document: 'Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patients starting hormone therapy: 5-year follow-up results from the STAMPEDE randomised trial (NCT00268476)'.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: On page 6, in the first paragraph of the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'Median time on abiraterone in the SOC + AAP group was 29 months (IQR 12-71)'.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The median on-treatment duration in months for the control arm (SOC-alone) is not explicitly reported in the document. While the control arm received 'lifelong ADT', a specific median duration of this treatment in months is not provided.

NCT.evidence: Found in the title of the article on page 1: "STAMPEDE randomised trial (NCT00268476)" and confirmed in the 'ETHICS STATEMENT' section on page 12: "The trial identification for STAMPEDE is NCT00268476 (clinicaltrials.gov)".

PubMed ID.evidence: The document provides a DOI (10.1002/ijc.34018) but no explicit PubMed ID.

Trial Name.evidence: Extracted from the main title of the article on page 1: "5-year follow-up results from the STAMPEDE randomised trial".

Author.evidence: The first author listed on page 1 is Nicholas D. James, followed by a long list of co-authors. Using 'et al.' is appropriate for such a long list.

Year.evidence: Found in the accepted date on page 1: "Accepted: 22 February 2022" and the journal publication year in the footer on page 1: "Int. J. Cancer. 2022;151:422-434.".

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is a multi-page article with extensive sections including introduction, methods, results, and discussion, indicating it is a full publication, not just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: Found in the 'KEYWORDS' section on page 3: "phase III". Also, reference 15 on page 13 mentions "a randomised controlled phase 3 trial".

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The title of the article on page 1 explicitly states: "5-year follow-up results from the STAMPEDE randomised trial". The 'What's new?' section on page 3 also mentions "long-term results".

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: On page 7, right column, first paragraph, it states: "Reported use of docetaxel within 1 year after first progression was higher among patients allocated to SOC + AAP (32% SOC-alone and 40% SOC + AAP, Chi-square P = .048)." The 'SOC + AAP' group is the treatment arm, and the corresponding percentage is 40%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: On page 7, right column, first paragraph, it states: "Reported use of docetaxel within 1 year after first progression was higher among patients allocated to SOC + AAP (32% SOC-alone and 40% SOC + AAP, Chi-square P = .048)." The 'SOC-alone' group is the control arm, and the corresponding percentage is 32%.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: Androgen receptor pathway targeting agent (Abiraterone acetate) and Corticosteroid (Prednisolone)
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The experimental arm (Treatment Arm 1) is 'SOC + AAP', where AAP stands for 'abiraterone acetate + prednisone/prednisolone' (page 1, Abbreviations; page 3, Results). The document states that abiraterone, enzalutamide, and apalutamide 'work by targeting the androgen receptor axis' (page 10, Discussion), classifying abiraterone acetate as an androgen receptor pathway targeting agent. Prednisolone is a corticosteroid.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The document mentions that 94% of patients were 'newly-diagnosed' (synchronous) in Table 1 on page 5. However, Section 4, 'Discussion' on page 10 states: 'Most patients in STAMPEDE had de novo metastatic disease... these trial data could not be used to explore whether there is a differential treatment effect by these characteristics.' This indicates that while synchronous patients were included, the results were not reported based on prognostic groups specifically for synchronous vs. metachronous status.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The document mentions that 6% of patients were 'relapsing' (potentially metachronous) in Table 1 on page 5. However, Section 4, 'Discussion' on page 10 states: 'Most patients in STAMPEDE had de novo metastatic disease... these trial data could not be used to explore whether there is a differential treatment effect by these characteristics.' This indicates that while metachronous patients were included, the results were not reported based on prognostic groups specifically for synchronous vs. metachronous status.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: On page 3, the text states: 'metastatic disease risk group: 48% high, 44% low, 8% unassessable'. It further reports: 'This was similar in low-risk (HR = 0.55; 95% CI: 0.41-0.76) and high-risk (HR = 0.54; 95% CI: 0.43-0.69) patients.' Table 3 on page 8 explicitly presents 'Primary and Secondary outcomes, by metastatic disease risk group using LATITUDE criteria' with separate columns for 'Low-risk' and 'High-risk' groups, confirming reporting by high volume prognostic groups.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: On page 3, the text states: 'metastatic disease risk group: 48% high, 44% low, 8% unassessable'. It further reports: 'This was similar in low-risk (HR = 0.55; 95% CI: 0.41-0.76) and high-risk (HR = 0.54; 95% CI: 0.43-0.69) patients.' Table 3 on page 8 explicitly presents 'Primary and Secondary outcomes, by metastatic disease risk group using LATITUDE criteria' with separate columns for 'Low-risk' and 'High-risk' groups, confirming reporting by low volume prognostic groups.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The title of the article states "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patients starting hormone therapy". The abstract on page 2 also mentions "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE..." and the main text on page 3 confirms "SOC + abiraterone acetate + prednisolone" as the intervention group.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a report on a single randomized controlled trial (STAMPEDE). It discusses patient risk groups (e.g., high-risk, low-risk metastatic disease) but does not provide an 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE)' for randomized controlled trials in general or for the trial itself in terms of methodological quality or risk of bias. This type of assessment is typically found in systematic reviews or meta-analyses, not individual trial reports.

Type of Therapy.value: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patients starting hormone therapy (Androgen Deprivation Therapy)
Type of Therapy.evidence: The title of the paper is "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patients starting hormone therapy". The abstract on page 2 states "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE... in men starting long-term hormone therapy for prostate cancer. ...Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy." Section 2.3 Procedures on page 4 further details that "All patients received lifelong ADT... Patients allocated to the SOC + AAP group were also planned to receive abiraterone (1000 mg daily) with prednisolone (5 mg daily)." This indicates the intervention is Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone in addition to standard hormone therapy (ADT).

Treatment Class.evidence: The title of the paper is 'Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patients starting hormone therapy'. The abstract states 'men starting long-term hormone therapy for prostate cancer' and 'Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy'. The keywords also include 'hormone therapy'. The study compares standard-of-care (androgen deprivation therapy) with standard-of-care plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in the main text on page 6: "Median survival was ... 79 months (IQR 33, not reached) in the SOC + AAP group". SOC + AAP refers to the experimental arm.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in the main text on page 6: "Median survival was 46 months (IQR 25, 92) in the SOC-alone group". SOC-alone refers to the control arm.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Found on page 8, in the "DISCUSSION" section: "The worst reported grade of toxicity was similar between randomised groups (P = .29, Table S2) with grade 3 toxicity for ... 20 (7%) in the SOC + AAP group, and no grade 4 or 5 toxicity." Since no grade 4 or 5 toxicity was reported, grade 3 toxicity represents grade 3 or higher. This is assumed to be all-cause as not specified otherwise.

Found on page 8, in the "DISCUSSION" section: "The worst reported grade of toxicity was similar between randomised groups (P = .29, Table S2) with grade 3 toxicity for 12 (9%) in the SOC-alone group patients ... and no grade 4 or 5 toxicity." Since no grade 4 or 5 toxicity was reported, grade 3 toxicity represents grade 3 or higher. This is assumed to be all-cause as not specified otherwise.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: The document reports "grade 3 toxicity" but does not explicitly specify if it is treatment-related or all-cause. In the absence of explicit mention of "treatment-related grade 3 or higher" events, this information is considered not found.
